issuesFound.fix:
Line 428: <th scope="col">Feature</th>
       Line 429: <th scope="col">Starter</th>
       Line 430: <th scope="col">Pro</th>
       Line 431: <th scope="col">Enterprise</th>

issuesFound.title: missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty. A descriptive title is required for users to identify the page's purpose and distinguish it from other open tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design and Productivity Tools</title>

issuesFound.title: toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button (hamburger menu) lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 34: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: broken ARIA reference: element references id that does not exist
issuesFound.explanation: The "Resources" button has aria-controls="resources-submenu", but the target <ul> on line 64 has no id attribute to match.
issuesFound.fix: Line 64: <ul id="resources-submenu" role="list" hidden>

issuesFound.title: landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple <nav> elements on the page, but this instance (containing account links) lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish it from the main navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: <nav aria-label="Account">

issuesFound.title: image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image is missing an alt attribute. While the parent div has aria-hidden="true", the itself should have alt="" if decorative or a description if it conveys information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 141:

issuesFound.title: non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Users navigating by links alone will not know what they are reading more about.
issuesFound.fix: Line 130: Read more about signing up (no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: heading level skip
issuesFound.explanation: The heading structure jumps from <h2> (line 204) to <h5> (line 208) within the "How it works" section, skipping levels h3 and h4.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 208:

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The "First name" input is missing a <label for> or wrapping <label>. The visible text is not programmatically associated with the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 668: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" required aria-labelledby="contact-first-name-label"> (Note: Ensure label has id="contact-first-name-label")

issuesFound.title: missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "First name" input collects personal data but lacks an autocomplete attribute (SC 1.3.5).
issuesFound.fix: Line 668: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> elements in the table header row lack a scope="col" attribute. In a complex comparison table, explicit scope is required for screen readers to associate headers with data cells.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 428: <th scope="col">Feature</th>
       Line 429: <th scope="col">Starter</th>
       Line 430: <th scope="col">Pro</th>
       Line 431: <th scope="col">Enterprise</th>

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The outline is explicitly removed for elements that have focus but do not match the :focus-visible criteria. This prevents users who rely on standard :focus styles (in older browsers or specific configurations) from seeing where the keyboard focus is located.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the default focus outline. While a box-shadow is provided on line 517, box-shadow often disappears in Windows High Contrast Mode, leaving the element with no visible focus indicator for those users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: `#search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs, selects, and textareas explicitly remove the outline on focus. The provided box-shadow replacement is insufficient for users in high-contrast environments where shadows are not rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: `.form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly set to 28px by 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of at least 24x24 pixels of target size only if there is sufficient spacing; however, as an icon button in a header, it fails the preferred 44x44px ergonomic target and lacks explicit spacing to prevent accidental activation of the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 524: `width: 44px;`, Line 525: `height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links img
issuesFound.explanation: The social media icons are set to 20px by 20px. While the parent anchor has a min-height of 44px, the horizontal width of the interactive area is constrained by the small image size and lack of horizontal padding, making it difficult for touch users to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1074: `width: 24px;`, Line 1074: `height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The button active state uses a transform (translateY) to simulate a press. This movement is not disabled or simplified within the global prefers-reduced-motion block, which only targets animation-duration and transition-duration.
issuesFound.fix: Line 398: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The primary hero button moves vertically on hover. This movement can be disorienting for users with vestibular disorders and is not explicitly disabled in the reduced motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic items shift upward on hover. This spatial movement lacks a specific override to ensure the element remains static for users who have requested reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 852: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards translate vertically on hover. While there is a media query on line 802, it uses `transform: none` but the original transition on line 792 still attempts to calculate the state change, and the hover rule itself should be guarded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 802: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transform: none; transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards shift on hover. The reduced motion block on line 922 should explicitly ensure no transform occurs to prevent motion sickness or distraction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 922: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card uses both a translation and a scale transform on hover. This complex motion is particularly problematic for sensitive users and requires a specific suppression.
issuesFound.fix: Line 941: `.pricing-card--featured:hover { box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses `var(--letter-spacing-tighter)` which is defined as -0.05em. Negative letter spacing causes characters to collapse into each other, significantly impairing readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 668: `.hero-content h1 { letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics values use `var(--letter-spacing-tighter)` (-0.05em). This makes large numbers harder to distinguish, especially when digits like '1' and '7' or '0' and '8' are placed close together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 857: `.stat-value { letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .feature-card a
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards have `text-decoration: none` set via the base `a` reset or component style. Although they have an arrow icon, the primary identification of the link in the block is its blue colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 594: `.feature-card a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .faq-item dd p a
issuesFound.explanation: Links within FAQ answers have no underline by default. They only gain an underline on hover (line 997). Users who cannot perceive the colour difference will not know the text is interactive until they interact with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 995: `.faq-item dd p a { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The root html element sets font-size to 12px. This causes all relative units (rem/em) for body text to be significantly smaller than the 16px minimum required for readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 216: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the default browser outline but does not define a visible replacement (like outline-color or box-shadow) within the same block. This leaves interactive elements without a visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 368: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the focus outline for users who do not trigger :focus-visible (such as some stylus or older browser users) without providing any visual alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 374: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This fails to visually hide the content, and because it lacks the standard clipping properties, it may interfere with the layout or be ignored by screen readers if it overlaps other content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 398: position: absolute; Line 399: width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses a transform transition to slide into view. There is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion block to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 458: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class defines a transition for the transform property (used on :active). This motion is not disabled or simplified for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 458: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary hero button uses a transform transition for a hover lift effect. This motion is not suppressed for users with motion sensitivities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 458: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform transition for a hover lift effect. While there is a hover override on line 885, the transition itself should be disabled to prevent animated movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 885: .feature-card:hover { border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "→" icon on feature links uses a transform transition. This small, repetitive motion can be distracting or triggering for some users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 934: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use a transform transition for a hover effect. The transition remains active even if the hover transform is removed, which can cause "ghost" movements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1098: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items use a transform transition. The transition property itself is not disabled in the reduced motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards use a transform transition for hover effects. The transition should be removed for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1290: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion icon uses a transform transition to rotate. This motion is not disabled for users with motion sensitivities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1504: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: rotate(90deg) !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly sets outline: none on focus. While it adds a box-shadow, the shadow uses a low-contrast alpha value (0.2) which may not be visible to users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the default outline on focus and replace it with a low-opacity box-shadow (0.2) that does not meet the visibility requirements for a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1565: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings use var(--letter-spacing-tight) which is -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing can cause characters to touch or overlap, significantly hindering readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 254: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses var(--letter-spacing-tighter) which is -0.05em. This extreme compression makes the text difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: Stat values use var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), which can make large numbers difficult to distinguish.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .plan-price
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing amounts use var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), which can make currency and digits blur together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary and --colour-focus-ring variables are set to #aaaaaa. When used on white backgrounds (like the focus ring offset or secondary text), the contrast ratio is 2.3:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 68: --colour-text-secondary: #717171; Line 101: --colour-focus-ring: #717171;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 36px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended minimum of 44px for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 655: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height/width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is only 28x28px, making it difficult for users with motor impairments or those using touch devices to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 703: width: 44px; Line 704: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links img height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The social media icons are set to 20x20px. Although the parent anchor has a min-height of 44px, the visual target is too small, leading to activation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: width: 24px; height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation, pricing cards, and FAQ answers have text-decoration: none. Without underlines, users who are colour blind may not be able to distinguish these links from the surrounding static text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: text-decoration: underline; Line 1218: text-decoration: underline; Line 1511: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The root html element sets scroll-behavior: smooth, but the subsequent :focus-visible rule on line 385 sets outline: none. While a focus indicator is defined for :focus-visible, the global reset on line 391 removes the default focus ring for all elements that do not match the :focus-visible criteria, potentially leaving keyboard users without a visible indicator in certain browsers or configurations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 391: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly sets outline: none on :focus. Although it adds a border-color and box-shadow, the box-shadow uses a low-contrast alpha value (0.2) which may not be visible to users with low vision, and the outline is completely suppressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 558: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls (input, select, textarea) explicitly remove the outline on focus. The replacement box-shadow uses a 0.2 opacity which provides insufficient visual contrast to serve as a reliable focus indicator for all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1044: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button has an explicit min-width and min-height of 44px, but the padding and internal content may result in a visual target that is difficult to activate. While it meets the 44px threshold, the surrounding context in the header (line 510) uses a gap of var(--space-6) (24px), but the button itself is at the absolute minimum limit.
issuesFound.fix: Line 498: .btn-icon { min-width: 48px; min-height: 48px; padding: var(--space-3); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is incorrectly implemented. It uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which means the text remains visible on screen and occupies layout space, failing its purpose as a visually-hidden utility for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view. There is no override within the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block on line 470 to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 471: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); position: absolute; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transition for the 'transform' property (line 668) and an active state that moves the button 1px (line 680). These movements are not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 471: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card transitions the 'transform' property on hover (line 768). While there is a partial override on line 777, it only targets the hover state and not the base transition timing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 777: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The product card transitions the 'transform' property on hover (line 895). The reduced motion block on line 905 only disables the hover transform but leaves the transition logic active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card transitions the 'transform' property (line 1015). The reduced motion block on line 1023 does not remove the transition duration.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1023: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em). Negative letter-spacing can cause characters to overlap, significantly impacting readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 618: .hero-content h1 { font-size: var(--font-size-6xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-extrabold); line-height: var(--line-height-none); letter-spacing: normal; color: var(--colour-neutral-900); text-wrap: balance; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics values use --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em). This reduces the legibility of large numbers, which are critical data points.
issuesFound.fix: Line 964: .stat-value { font-size: var(--font-size-5xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-black); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); line-height: var(--line-height-none); letter-spacing: normal; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: The variable --colour-text-secondary is set to #aaaaaa and used against --colour-bg-page (#ffffff). This produces a contrast ratio of 2.32:1, which is well below the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 68: --colour-text-secondary: #64748b;

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px on html
issuesFound.explanation: The base font-size on the html element is set to 12px. This forces all rem-based calculations to be smaller than the browser default (usually 16px), making body text harder to read for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-colour alternative: .header-inner > a:first-child
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link in the header removes text-decoration (underline) but does not provide a non-colour differentiator like a border or bolding to distinguish it as a link if the image fails to load or for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: .header-inner > a:first-child { display: flex; align-items: center; flex-shrink: 0; text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-color: transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-md); } Line 521: .header-inner > a:first-child:hover { text-decoration-color: currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-colour alternative: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links remove underlines. While they have a hover background colour, they rely on colour/background change alone to signal interactivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 561: #nav-menu > li > a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; } Line 562: #nav-menu > li > a:hover { border-bottom-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-colour alternative: .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the address tag in the footer have text-decoration: none. They only gain an underline on hover, meaning they are indistinguishable from static text in their default state for users who cannot perceive colour.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1084: .footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-colour alternative: .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: Legal links in the footer bottom bar remove underlines by default, relying on colour alone until hovered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1102: .footer-bottom > p a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible pseudo-class explicitly removes the default browser outline but does not define a replacement visible style (like a box-shadow or new outline) within the same rule or its overrides. This leaves keyboard users with no visual indication of which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for elements that receive focus via non-keyboard methods. If a browser fails to correctly trigger :focus-visible, or if a user is using a hybrid input device, the focus indicator is entirely suppressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; } /* Ensure :focus-visible provides a robust alternative above */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible but does not provide a replacement visual style within this block. While transitions are defined, they do not guarantee a high-contrast focus ring required for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline on focus. While it adds a box-shadow, the shadow uses a low-opacity blue (rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2)) which may not meet the contrast requirements for a functional focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 715: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly sets the outline to none and the offset to 0, ensuring that even browsers supporting focus-visible will not render a standard focus ring for the search input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 722: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Global form inputs remove the outline on focus and replace it with a low-opacity box-shadow (0.2 alpha). This does not provide a sufficiently clear visual affordance for users with low vision or those in high-glare environments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1184: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is defined with "position: static", "width: auto", and "overflow: visible". This fails to hide the content visually, meaning "screen-reader only" text will be visible to all users, cluttering the UI.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has an explicit min-height of 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 Level AA requirement of at least 24x24 pixels for interactive targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 628: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button variant sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is too small for reliable activation by users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 642: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has a min-height and min-width of 28px. While above 24px, it is significantly below the 44px recommended size and lacks sufficient surrounding padding to prevent accidental activation of adjacent elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 675: .nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is explicitly set to 28px. This creates a narrow vertical target that is difficult for users with limited dexterity to click or tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 703: #search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button is restricted to 28x28 pixels. This is a small target for a primary action within the header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 728: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view. There is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion block to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card uses a transform transition on hover. While there is a media query on line 844, it only targets the hover state and not the base transition, which can cause stuttering or unexpected movement during reflow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 834: .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The product card animates a translateY movement on hover. There is no corresponding prefers-reduced-motion rule to neutralize this movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 995: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing causes characters from adjacent words to touch or overlap, significantly hindering readability for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 214: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing to -0.05em. Reducing the space between characters makes it harder for users to distinguish individual letters, failing readability best practices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 215: body { letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: :root (--letter-spacing-tighter)
issuesFound.explanation: The variable --letter-spacing-tighter is set to -0.05em and is applied to headings (h1) and stats. This reduces legibility of large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 134: --letter-spacing-tighter: 0;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: :root (--letter-spacing-tight)
issuesFound.explanation: The variable --letter-spacing-tight is set to -0.025em and is applied to all headings (h1-h6). This tightens character tracking and reduces readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 135: --letter-spacing-tight: 0;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) used against the white background (#ffffff) results in a contrast ratio of 2.32:1. This is well below the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 74: --colour-text-secondary: #636363;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global 'a' selector removes text-decoration. In the .prose sections or body text, links will only be distinguishable by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 241: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px. This makes the base text size for the entire document significantly smaller than the recommended 16px, forcing users to zoom.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: line-height below 1.5
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraphs (p) have line-height set to 1. This is too tight for body text and makes it difficult for users to track lines of text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 236: p { line-height: 1.5; text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible pseudo-class explicitly removes the default browser outline but does not define a replacement visible style (like a box-shadow or new outline) within the same rule or its overrides. This leaves keyboard users with no visual indication of which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for elements that receive focus via non-keyboard methods. If a browser fails to correctly trigger :focus-visible, or if a user is using a hybrid input device, the focus indicator is entirely suppressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; } /* Ensure :focus-visible provides a robust alternative above */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible but does not provide a replacement visual style within this block. While transitions are defined, they do not guarantee a high-contrast focus ring required for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline on focus. While it adds a box-shadow, the shadow uses a low-opacity blue (rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2)) which may not meet the contrast requirements for a functional focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 715: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly sets the outline to none and the offset to 0, ensuring that even browsers supporting focus-visible will not render a standard focus ring for the search input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 722: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Global form inputs remove the outline on focus and replace it with a low-opacity box-shadow (0.2 alpha). This does not provide a sufficiently clear visual affordance for users with low vision or those in high-glare environments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1184: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.fix: Line 1505: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the focus outline, preventing keyboard users from seeing which question is currently focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1645: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the default focus outline. The provided box-shadow uses a low-contrast alpha (0.2) which is insufficient as a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs explicitly remove the focus-visible outline, which is a direct failure of focus visibility requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1696: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px and the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized at 20px by 20px, making them very difficult for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 673: min-width: 44px; Line 674: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle is sized at 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: min-height: 44px; Line 706: min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, which is significantly smaller than the 44px recommended for reliable touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 756: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, creating a small target area for users on mobile devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 781: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 805: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is only 28px square, making it a difficult target for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 838: width: 44px; Line 839: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons have a min-height of 28px, which does not meet the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons are only 28px high, which is a small target for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1755: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets width and height to 'auto' and position to 'static', which fails to visually hide the content while keeping it accessible. Furthermore, the .not-sr-only class (Line 475) is used to hide content but does not use the correct properties to ensure it is hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: width: 1px; height: 1px; position: absolute; Line 446: clip: rect(0 0 0 0); clip-path: inset(50%);

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for its appearance, but there is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion block to disable this movement.
issuesFound.fix: (Inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block) .skip-link { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons use a transform transition on the :active state (Line 620), but this motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: (Inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block) .btn:active { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex perspective transform transition that can cause vestibular distress, and the reduced-motion block on line 975 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 976: .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform transition on hover, but the reduced-motion override on line 1036 only handles the hover state, not the base transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1036: .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use a transform transition on hover, but the reduced-motion override on line 1246 only handles the hover state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1246: .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: Stat items use a transform transition on hover, but the reduced-motion override on line 1326 only handles the hover state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1326: .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards use a transform transition on hover, but the reduced-motion override on line 1436 only handles the hover state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1436: .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion icon uses a rotation transition that is not disabled in the reduced-motion block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1648: .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary text colour (--colour-text-secondary) used on white backgrounds (--colour-bg-page) has a contrast ratio of 2.3:1, which is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for standard text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 78: --colour-text-secondary: #707070;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: The error colour (--colour-error) used for required markers on white backgrounds has a contrast ratio of 2.4:1, making it difficult for users to identify required fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 66: --colour-error: #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global reset removes underlines from all links. In body text (like .prose or .faq-item dd p), this relies entirely on colour to identify links, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour vision deficiency.
issuesFound.fix: Line 251: a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text on .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer address links explicitly remove underlines, making them indistinguishable from static text for some users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: .footer-brand address a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text on .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom links remove underlines, relying only on a slight colour change that is insufficient for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: .footer-bottom > p a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting a negative word-spacing value causes words to crowd together, making text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 239: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing reduces the space between characters, which can cause letters to overlap or blur together for many readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 240: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a box-shadow or border within the same block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.explanation: Explicitly removing the outline on the :focus pseudo-class can leave keyboard users without a focus indicator in browsers that do not fully support :focus-visible.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `setComparisonTableExpanded` function toggles the visibility of the pricing comparison table but uses a custom `setExpanded` helper that is not defined in the global scope or imported, and the logic for the trigger element does not explicitly call `setAttribute('aria-expanded', ...)` within the function body. Screen reader users will not be notified that the table content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); table.removeAttribute('hidden');`; Line 1155: `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); table.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function updates the visual state and layout of the product grid but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the button that was just clicked. Keyboard and screen reader users will not receive confirmation of which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true'); qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { if(b !== btn) b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false'); });`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product grid view mode is toggled between 'grid' and 'list', the visual layout changes significantly, but no ARIA live region announcement is made to inform non-sighted users of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835: `liveRegion.announce(mode === 'list' ? 'Switched to list view' : 'Switched to grid view');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `newsletterForm` validation logic checks for a valid email and applies visual error classes but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element. Screen reader users will not be notified that the field contains an error when they navigate back to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2545: `_emailEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter form is successfully submitted or the user corrects an error, the `aria-invalid` attribute is not removed or set to `false`. This results in the field continuing to be announced as "invalid" even after the data is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2565: `_emailEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the navigation toggle and drawer but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button during initialisation. If the attribute is missing from the source HTML, the initial state of the menu is ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click listener on non-native element
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a click listener to the `zoneEl` (a `<div>`) to trigger the file input, but it does not provide a corresponding `keydown` handler for the 'Enter' or 'Space' keys. Keyboard users cannot activate the upload zone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if(e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Programmatic focus not returned on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent` banner acts as a modal dialog and traps focus, but when the "Accept" or "Decline" buttons are clicked, focus is not explicitly returned to the element that triggered the banner or a logical starting point like the skip link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: `_hideBanner(); const skipLink = qs(SELECTORS.skipLinks); if(skipLink) skipLink.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `animateHeight` function, used by the FAQ accordion and other components to show/hide content, does not update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger element. Screen reader users will not be notified that the content has expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `el.removeAttribute('hidden'); el.style.height = ''; if(trigger) trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');` Line 1156: `el.setAttribute('hidden', ''); el.style.height = ''; if(trigger) trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the product grid layout between 'grid' and 'list' modes but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the buttons. Keyboard and screen reader users will not know which view mode is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode)); });`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the visible count of products is updated in the DOM, but this change is not announced via an ARIA live region. Users of assistive technology will not know how many results matched their filter criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: `_countBadge.textContent = String(count); liveRegion.announce(count + " products found");`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds a CSS error class to invalid fields but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen reader users will not be alerted that a specific input contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3345: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); field.classList.add(CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes the error class but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. Assistive technology may continue to report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3340: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); field.classList.remove(CSS.fieldInvalid);`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function for the FAQ accordion sets the initial visibility of panels based on data attributes but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger buttons for items that are closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2885: `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', item.open ? 'true' : 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click listener on non-native element
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` attaches a click listener to a `<div>` used as a drop zone but does not provide a keydown handler for the 'Enter' or 'Space' keys. Keyboard-only users cannot activate the file selection dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3785: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The dismiss button for toast notifications uses a '×' character but lacks a descriptive `aria-label`. Screen reader users will hear "times button" or "X button" instead of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': 'Dismiss notification' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner is injected into the DOM and shown as a modal dialog, but focus is not programmatically moved into the banner when it appears. Keyboard users may continue interacting with the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4585: `_bannerEl.removeAttribute('hidden'); const firstBtn = _bannerEl.querySelector('button'); if(firstBtn) firstBtn.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is dismissed, focus is not returned to the element that triggered it (or a logical starting point). This causes the focus to be lost, forcing keyboard users to tab through the entire page again.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4545: `_hideBanner(); if(_triggerEl) _triggerEl.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Progress bar missing required ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateProgress` function in the multi-step wizard updates the width of a fill element but the parent container lacks `role="progressbar"` and the necessary `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, and `aria-valuemax` attributes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3545: `<div data-wizard-progress role="progressbar" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100" aria-valuenow="0">`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1601: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The NewsletterForm renders an error message when the email is invalid, but the TextInput is not programmatically linked to the error message, so the error won't be announced when the input gains focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2168: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter submit button has a loading state ('isSubmitting'), but it does not use aria-busy to inform assistive technologies that a process is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>

issuesFound.title: toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown component uses the 'isOpen' state to toggle the visibility of a sub-menu, but the trigger button does not communicate this state to assistive technologies. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1004: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The search icon (🔍) inside the submit button is purely decorative as the button already has a visually hidden label "Submit search". Without aria-hidden, screen readers may announce the emoji character.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: 🔍

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually or from screen readers. This fails to provide a proper screen-reader-only text technique, breaking WCAG 1.3.1 and 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector uses transform: translateY(1px); for spatial movement without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. This can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide content from the visual viewport but may still be read by screen readers. The current implementation does not hide the content visually.

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static and clip: auto, making content visible on screen. Screen reader users will hear duplicated content, while sighted users see redundant text.

issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a 3D transform animation on hover without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort or distraction from the movement.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a discount code is invalid, the input field is not marked with aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users may not be aware of the invalid state of the input.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onApply function, after validation fails, set the input's aria-invalid attribute to 'true'. Also, clear it to 'false' when the input becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is removed, the change in the filter state is not announced to screen readers. Users who rely on screen readers may not know that the filter has been removed.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onChipClick function of activeFilterChips, after removing the chip, announce the removal using liveRegion.announce.

issuesFound.title: Overlay click event without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay that closes the mobile menu only has a click event listener and is not focusable. Keyboard users cannot activate the overlay to close the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Add a tabindex="0" to the overlay element and add a keydown event listener that closes the menu on Enter or Space key press.

fpTitles: Discount code input validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
Active filter chip removal not announced to screen readers
Overlay click event without keyboard support

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open and close functions set aria-expanded on _trigger, but the toggle() function at line 2000 does not. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (name, role, value) because screen-reader users won't be notified of the state change when the hamburger menu is toggled via this function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: function toggle() { _open ? close() : open(); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', _open ? 'false' : 'true'); }

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's _onToggleClick function changes the billing period but does not update aria-pressed on the toggle switch element. Users who rely on screen readers won't know the toggle's current state (month/year).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: function _onToggleClick() { const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year'; setPeriod(next); setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', next === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false'); }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen-reader users won't know the filter results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: After the liveRegion.announce call, add: const liveEl = document.getElementById('filter-status') || createElement('div', {id:'filter-status', role:'status', 'aria-live':'polite', class:'sr-only'}); document.body.appendChild(liveEl); liveEl.textContent = totalMatches === 0 ? 'No products found' : `${totalMatches} products shown`;

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid on the input element when validation fails. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (error identification) for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In the else block of _renderResult, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init function registers dropdowns but does not set aria-expanded="false" on triggers that are closed on page load. Screen readers may report an incorrect state on initial focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: In the register function, after setting aria-haspopup, add: if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) { setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); }

fpTitles: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function `markInvalid` in the `srUtils` module does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid form fields. Screen reader users are not informed of the invalid state of the field, which can prevent them from correcting errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1510: In the `markInvalid` function, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the `addDescribedBy` call.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs do not have ARIA state for active tab
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs (buttons with data-filter-value) do not have an ARIA attribute (such as aria-pressed or aria-selected) to indicate the active tab. Screen reader users may not know which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2830: In the _syncTabAriaStates function, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` or `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel element is not programmatically labeled by its trigger button. Screen reader users may not hear a descriptive name for the expanded panel, causing confusion about its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: After setting the panel id, also set the trigger id and link them: const triggerId = srUtils.ensureId(trigger, 'dropdown-trigger'); setAttr(panel, 'aria-labelledby', triggerId);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (off-canvas menu) lacks an accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby). Screen reader users navigating inside the drawer will not hear a clear label identifying the region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: In the mobileNav.init function, after obtaining _drawer, add: setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-label', 'Main navigation menu');

issuesFound.title: Testimonial carousel slides missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: Each slide in the testimonials carousel is missing an accessible label. Screen reader users cannot distinguish between slides when they change, as the slides only have generic labels like "Testimonial 1 of 4".
issuesFound.fix: Line 3250: In the _slides mapping, enhance each slide object to include a descriptive label from data attributes or content: slide.el.setAttribute('aria-label', `Testimonial from ${authorName}`);

issuesFound.title: Product grid missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The product grid container lacks an accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby). Screen reader users navigating within the grid may not understand its purpose as a collection of product cards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In productFilter.init, after obtaining _gridEl, add: setAttr(_gridEl, 'aria-label', 'Products list');

issuesFound.title: Pricing cards missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: Each pricing card (plan) element lacks an accessible name. Screen reader users cannot quickly identify the plan type (e.g., "Pro plan") when navigating through the cards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2700: In planDataParser.parse, after obtaining the plan id, add: setAttr(el, 'aria-label', `${capitalise(id)} plan`);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel missing aria-labelledby on initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion panels are not linked to their trigger buttons via aria-labelledby during initialization. This can cause screen readers to not announce the relationship between question and answer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3080: In faqAccordion.init, inside the loop that applies initial ARIA states, ensure: setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-labelledby', srUtils.ensureId(item.trigger, 'faq-trigger'));

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions listbox missing aria-activedescendant synchronization
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestions listbox does not update aria-activedescendant on the input when keyboard navigating through suggestions. Screen reader users may not hear which suggestion is currently highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1550: In suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex, ensure the input's aria-activedescendant is set: if (_inputEl && index >= 0) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-activedescendant', items[index].id);

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications missing aria-live politeness adjustment
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications use a static aria-live value without considering the message type. Error toasts should be assertive to interrupt, while success/info toasts should be polite.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: In toastManager.show, set the aria-live attribute based on type: const live = type === 'error' ? 'assertive' : 'polite'; setAttr(toast, 'aria-live', live);

fpTitles: Testimonial carousel slides missing aria-label
Product grid missing aria-label
Pricing cards missing aria-label
Toast notifications missing aria-live politeness adjustment

issuesFound.title: Filter tab container missing role="tablist" and buttons missing role="tab"
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab container lacks role="tablist" and individual buttons lack role="tab". Screen reader users will not recognize the widget as a tabbed interface, hindering navigation and understanding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In productFilter init, add: if (_tabsEl) _tabsEl.setAttribute('role', 'tablist'); and for each button with data-filter-value, set role="tab".

issuesFound.title: Filter chip additions/removals not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is added or removed in activeFilterChips.render(), no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the change in filter state, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In activeFilterChips.render(), after updating the chip list, add: liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${action}ed: ${label}`);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel missing role="menu" and links missing role="menuitem"
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu panel and its child links lack appropriate ARIA menu roles (role="menu", role="menuitem"). Screen reader users may not understand the navigation structure and cannot use menu-specific commands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: In dropdownMenu.register, add: panel.setAttribute('role', 'menu'); and for each link inside, set role="menuitem".

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing role="navigation"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer element (_drawer) lacks an explicit role="navigation". Screen reader users may not identify it as a navigation landmark, making it harder to find.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: In mobileNav.init, add: if (_drawer) _drawer.setAttribute('role', 'navigation');

issuesFound.title: Product grid container missing role="list" and cards missing role="listitem"
issuesFound.explanation: The product grid container (_gridEl) and individual product cards lack ARIA list roles. Screen reader users cannot perceive the list structure, reducing context for the number and order of items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In productFilter.init, add: _gridEl.setAttribute('role', 'list'); and for each product card, set role="listitem".

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion container missing region role and label
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion container (_containerEl) lacks role="region" and an accessible label. Screen reader users cannot easily locate and identify the FAQ section as a distinct region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: In faqAccordion.init, add: _containerEl.setAttribute('role', 'region'); _containerEl.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Frequently Asked Questions');

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs not keyboard navigable with Arrow keys
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons cannot be navigated using Arrow keys. Keyboard-only users cannot move between tabs without using Tab, breaking expected tablist keyboard interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In productFilter.init, add a keydown event listener to _tabsEl to handle ArrowLeft/ArrowRight and move focus between tabs.
