issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function changes the billing period but does not update aria-pressed on the toggle button. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the button's state (monthly/annual), violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, period === 'year');

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates visible card count and grid but does not announce changes via an ARIA live region. Screen-reader users are not informed when filter results change, degrading experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3700: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates all plan prices without a live-region announcement. Screen-reader users are not notified when prices switch between monthly and annual billing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields with error classes but does not set aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify invalid fields, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4200: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', !result.valid);

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger does not have initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not set an initial aria-expanded value on the menu trigger button. Screen-reader users may not know the button controls an expandable menu on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2400: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not have initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init function does not set initial aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons. Screen-reader users are not informed of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2600: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the product grid and count badge without updating an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the change in product count or filtering results, degrading their experience.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the product grid, update a live region with the count of products found. Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` inside the applyFilters function after the `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` call.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates displayed prices without announcing the change to screen readers. Users who rely on screen readers may not know that prices have changed, degrading their experience.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the prices, update a live region with the new billing period. Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` inside the setPeriod function after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial state of the menu, degrading their experience.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav init function, set the initial aria-expanded state. Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the trigger element is assigned.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle buttons missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu toggle buttons do not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial state of the dropdowns, degrading their experience.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu init function, set the initial aria-expanded state for each trigger. Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the register function after setting aria-haspopup.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users may not be alerted to validation errors, blocking access.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, set aria-invalid on the field when invalid. Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true');` inside the _renderResult function.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The togglePlay function toggles carousel auto-play but does not update aria-expanded on the play/pause button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the state change, blocking access.
issuesFound.fix: In the togglePlay function, update aria-expanded on the play button. Add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-expanded', _playing ? 'true' : 'false');` inside the togglePlay function.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _fetchAndRender function updates search suggestions without announcing the count to screen readers via a live region. Screen reader users may not know new suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions, update a live region with the count. Add `liveRegion.announce(`${results.length} suggestion${results.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} available`);` inside the _fetchAndRender function after setting _searchState.suggestions.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion init function does not set aria-expanded on FAQ triggers based on initial open state. Screen reader users may not know the initial expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the faqAccordion init function, set aria-expanded on each trigger based on item.open. Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', item.open ? 'true' : 'false');` inside the loop that applies initial ARIA states.

fpTitles: aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, close, and toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2173: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 2189: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`. Line 2197: modify toggle to set based on new state.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs lack aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function toggles an active CSS class but does not set aria-pressed on filter tab buttons. Screen reader users cannot perceive which filter is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2906: replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: Product count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product count but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change, degrading experience (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2879: inside the filterAnimation.run callback after _updateCountBadge, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : \`Showing ${_productFilterState.activeCategory} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price displays without a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified when prices change due to billing period toggle, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3397: after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields with error classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not informed of field error states, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4246: in the else block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. In the valid block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the hamburger button. Screen reader users may not know the button controls an expandable menu, degrading experience (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2160: after `_initialised = true;` add `if (_trigger && !_trigger.hasAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the search input when the suggestions panel is hidden. Screen reader users are not informed the input controls a popup, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2546: after `_initialised = true;` add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function updates numeric values without a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed when statistic numbers change, degrading experience (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 5448: inside the onComplete callback, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Statistic updated to ${el.textContent}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users will not be notified of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1834: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1848: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function changes the active billing period but does not set aria-pressed on the period toggle buttons. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2779: inside the setPeriod function, after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add code to update aria-pressed on all period buttons: `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btn.getAttribute('data-period') === period); });`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen‑reader users are not informed when filtering results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2242: after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`${totalMatches} products found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen‑reader users may not know the initial state of the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1819: after `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Click handler on non‑button element without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._onToggleClick() function is attached to a non‑button element (the toggle track) via a click event listener, but there is no keydown handler for Enter and Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2788: add a keydown event listener to `_toggleEl` that checks for `e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE` and calls `_onToggleClick()`.

fpTitles: Click handler on non‑button element without keyboard support

issuesFound.title: FormGroup does not pass required prop to input

issuesFound.fix: The fix requires updating the FormGroup component to pass the required prop to its child input. Since the child is passed via children prop, the fix must be implemented by the parent component using FormGroup. Example fix for a usage: In the NewsletterForm component (line 1764), update the TextInput to include the required attribute.

issuesFound.title: Transform on hover without reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` rule applies a `transform: translateY(-1px)` on hover, creating motion. No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disables this transform, so users who request reduced motion cannot avoid the animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

fpTitles: Transform on hover without reduced‑motion override

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class defines a min-height of 36 px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch‑target size of 44 × 44 px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

fpTitles: Touch target too small on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline (outline: none) but does not provide any visible alternative such as a box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline. Keyboard users lose the visual focus cue, violating WCAG 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector inherits the global outline removal and only changes outline‑offset, providing no visible focus cue for elements that use the .focus-ring-inset class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible rule sets only outline‑color while the outline itself has been removed globally, resulting in no visible focus indication for elements using .focus-ring-brand.
issuesFound.fix: Line 320: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover selector applies a transform (translateY(-1px)) for a hover animation, but there is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies a transform (translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)) without a prefers‑reduced‑motion guard, causing motion that cannot be disabled for users who request reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) and does not provide any other visible focus style (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Keyboard users lose the focus indicator for all interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` rule also removes the outline (`outline: none`) without supplying an alternative visual cue, further eliminating focus visibility for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` and only offsets it, leaving no visible focus style for button elements, which blocks keyboard users from seeing focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 727: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn (min-height 32px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` component defines `min-height: 32px`, which is smaller than the WCAG‑recommended 44 × 44 px touch target, making it hard for users with limited dexterity to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: `.btn { min-height: 44px; /* increased to meet target size */`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min-height 36px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` variant sets `min-height: 36px`, still below the 44 px minimum, resulting in insufficient touch target area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 813: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; /* increased to meet target size */`

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; overflow: visible; clip: auto;` which does **not** hide the element from assistive technologies and also fails to hide it visually, breaking the intended screen‑reader‑only technique.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the rule with a proper implementation:  

  Line 465: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0 0 0 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes a `transition: … transform var(--transition-fast);` which animates spatial movement, but there is no `prefers-reduced-motion` guard disabling it, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override:  

  Line 698 (new block after existing rule): `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` animates `transform: translateY(-1px);` without a reduced‑motion media query, violating users’ motion‑sensitivity preferences.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion guard for this selector:  

  Line 1213 (new block after existing rule): `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min-height 36px)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus style removes the outline (`outline: none`) and does not provide an alternative visual cue, leaving focus invisible for all button elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility removes the outline without adding any visible replacement, so elements using the class lose their focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule removes the outline and only changes the outline colour, but because the outline is already set to none by the base selector, no visible focus indicator remains.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector applies a transform: translateY(1px) on :active, creating motion. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this transform, so users who prefer reduced motion still experience the animation.

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit` the email address is validated with `validationRules.test(emailVal, ['required','email'])`. When the test fails the code shows an error message but never sets `aria‑invalid` on the `<input type="email">` nor links the error message with `aria-describedby`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect that the field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls when drawer has no id
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init` the trigger button receives `aria‑controls` only if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id` the button has no relationship to the controlled region, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger missing aria‑controls when panel lacks id
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register` the trigger button is given `aria‑controls` only if the panel already has an `id`. Panels created without an `id` leave the button without a reference, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing._syncToggleState` updates the visual state of the period buttons but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users of assistive technology may miss the change.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button selected by `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn` is shown/hidden but never receives an `aria‑label`, so a screen‑reader cannot convey its purpose.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel navigation buttons lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s previous/next buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]`, `[data-carousel-next]`) are identified only by data attributes and have no `aria‑label`, making their purpose unclear to assistive technology.

fpTitles: Newsletter form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls when drawer has no id
Dropdown‑menu trigger missing aria‑controls when panel lacks id

issuesFound.title: Search input missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` element (selected via `SELECTORS.searchInput`) is given ARIA attributes for autocomplete and controls but never receives an accessible name (no `<label>`, no `aria-label`, and no `aria-labelledby`). Screen‑reader users cannot identify the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Add an accessible name, e.g. `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', 'Search site');` or associate a `<label>` element with the input.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The “scroll to top” button (`SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn`) is given `aria-hidden` and hidden attributes but never receives an accessible name. Screen‑reader users cannot discover its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', 'Scroll to top');` or include descriptive text inside the button.

issuesFound.title: Discount code input missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code `<input>` (`[data-discount-input]`) is used for entering a coupon but lacks a `<label>` element or `aria-label`/`aria-labelledby`. Assistive technologies cannot convey its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add a `<label for="discount-input">Discount code</label>` or `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', 'Discount code');`.

issuesFound.title: Carousel previous button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel “previous” navigation button (`[data-carousel-prev]`) is created without an accessible name. Users of assistive technology cannot know its function.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(prevBtn, 'aria-label', 'Previous testimonial');` or include visible text.

issuesFound.title: Carousel next button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel “next” navigation button (`[data-carousel-next]`) lacks an accessible name, making its purpose unclear to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(nextBtn, 'aria-label', 'Next testimonial');` or include visible text.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto‑play toggle button (`[data-carousel-play]`) is given `aria-pressed` but no accessible name, so its function (“pause” or “play”) is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(playBtn, 'aria-label', 'Pause automatic slide rotation');` and update the label when toggled.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` element (selected via `SELECTORS.searchInput`) receives no accessible name (no `<label>`, no `aria-label`, no `title`, and no `placeholder`). Screen‑reader users cannot identify the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Add an accessible name, e.g. `<label for="search-input">Search</label>` or `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', 'Search site')` in the `siteSearch.init` function.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The clear button (`SELECTORS.searchClear`) is created without an `aria-label` or visible text that conveys its purpose. Users of assistive technology cannot discover the button’s function.
issuesFound.fix: In `siteSearch.init`, after selecting `_clearBtn`, add `setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', 'Clear search')` or provide visible text such as “Clear”.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tab buttons lack proper tab semantics
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) are given `aria-pressed` but are not marked as tabs (`role="tab"`) and their container lacks `role="tablist"`. This prevents screen‑reader users from navigating the tab list correctly.
issuesFound.fix: In `productFilter.init`, add `setAttr(_tabsEl, 'role', 'tablist')` and for each tab button `setAttr(btn, 'role', 'tab')`. Also manage `aria-selected` instead of `aria-pressed` for tab state.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tab buttons use `aria-pressed` instead of `aria-selected`
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs are toggles, but they represent mutually exclusive selection (a tab list). Using `aria-pressed` is inappropriate; `aria-selected` should be used to indicate the active tab.
issuesFound.fix: Replace `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', ...)` with `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', ...)` and update the corresponding CSS/JS logic.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input missing label
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form selects the email field (`_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl)`) but does not associate a `<label>` or `aria-label`. Screen‑reader users cannot identify the field’s purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Add a `<label for="newsletter-email">Email address</label>` in the HTML or set `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-label', 'Email address')` during `newsletterForm.init`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product count updates not announced to screen readers (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters` updates the product count badge (`_updateCountBadge`) but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Users are unaware when the number of visible products changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and update its text inside `_updateCountBadge` with the new count.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function `mobileNav.toggle` does not set `aria-expanded` on the trigger when called programmatically (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle` method changes the navigation state by calling `open()` or `close()`, but when invoked directly (e.g., from other scripts) it does not ensure the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute reflects the new state, leading to a mismatch between visual state and ARIA.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `mobileNav.toggle`, after calling `open()` or `close()`, explicitly set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(_open))`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button for view mode (`data-view-mode`) does not set `aria-pressed` on all related buttons (JS‑B)
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` handler updates the clicked button’s `aria-pressed` but does not reset the attribute on the other view‑mode buttons, leaving multiple buttons with `aria-pressed="true"`. This violates the requirement that only the active toggle have `aria-pressed="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, iterate over all `[data-view-mode]` buttons and set `aria-pressed` to `true` for the active one and `false` for the others.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors in the contact form are not reflected with `aria-invalid` (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses `inlineValidator` for field validation, but the validation rules are attached only after the form is initialized; however, the custom error messages inserted by `contactForm._showError` do not set `aria-invalid` on the corresponding inputs. Consequently, assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `contactForm._onSubmit`, after detecting validation errors, call `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` for each invalid field, or ensure `inlineValidator.validate` is invoked for all fields before submission.  

These ten issue blocks satisfy the required minimum of eight distinct accessibility problems detected in the provided JavaScript code.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button when the navigation drawer is opened.
issuesFound.explanation: The function `open()` adds CSS classes and focus handling but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to reflect the opened state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: After `addClass(_drawer, …)` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (or use `toggleAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', true)`).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria‑expanded="true" on its trigger button when a dropdown panel is opened.
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function only removes the `hidden` attribute and adds a CSS class to the panel; it never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_openInstance`, after opening the panel call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close does not set aria‑expanded="false" on the FAQ question button when its answer panel is collapsed.
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item)` function hides the panel but does not reset the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, leaving the button in an indeterminate state for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing the item.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid visually but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid input.
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` helper returns a result, and `inlineValidator.validate` calls `_renderResult`, which only adds error classes and messages via `srUtils.markInvalid`. `markInvalid` does **not** set the required `aria-invalid` attribute, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markInvalid`, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (and ensure the attribute is removed on valid state).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator.validate does not clear `aria-invalid` on inputs that become valid.
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markValid` removes error classes but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`. Assistive technologies may still treat the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In `srUtils.markValid`, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or set to `"false"` if preferred).

issuesFound.title: Theme‑toggle elements (`[data‑theme‑toggle]`) are used as interactive buttons but lack an explicit `role="button"` attribute.
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher adds `aria‑pressed` and keyboard handling to generic elements, but without `role="button"` screen readers may not announce them as actionable controls.
issuesFound.fix: In the themeSwitcher `init` routine, after locating each toggle element, ensure `setAttr(btn, 'role', 'button');` (if the element is not already a `<button>`).

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button (`SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn`) is toggled with `aria‑hidden` but never receives an accessible name (`aria-label` or visible text).
issuesFound.explanation: The button is hidden/shown and announced via a live region, yet without an accessible name users of assistive technology cannot know its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the button has `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');` or include visible text inside the button.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not reset `aria‑pressed` on toggle buttons that act as switches (e.g., language selector).
issuesFound.explanation: While the function sets `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger, it does not update `aria‑pressed` for buttons that represent a two‑state toggle, potentially leaving the pressed state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: After closing, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` where appropriate (or ensure the button’s role includes `aria-pressed`).

fpTitles: inlineValidator.validate does not clear `aria-invalid` on inputs that become valid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and announces the change, but never updates aria‑expanded on the hamburger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know when the menu is opened.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the menu is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer when it is hidden
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes and unlocks scrolling, but the drawer element remains without aria‑hidden="true". Assistive technologies may still treat the hidden navigation as visible.
issuesFound.fix:
After removing the open classes, add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); (and remove it when opening).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack aria‑controls because the drawer has no id attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init() the code only adds aria‑controls to the trigger if the drawer already has an id. If the drawer lacks an id, the relationship is missing, breaking the accessible link between button and menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer receives a unique id (e.g., _drawer.id = `nav-drawer-${shortId()}`) before setting aria‑controls, or always set aria‑controls to the drawer’s generated id.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() makes the panel visible but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the dropdown.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not set aria‑hidden on the panel when it is hidden
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() removes CSS classes and hides the panel with setAttribute('hidden'), but does not set aria‑hidden="true". The panel may still be announced by assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix:
After hiding, add setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); (and remove it when opening).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() adds CSS classes and animates the answer panel, but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the FAQ item.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria‑hidden on the answer panel when it is collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() animates the panel closed and adds a hidden attribute, but does not set aria‑hidden="true". The hidden content may still be exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); when closing, and remove it when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() uses srUtils.markInvalid() which only adds aria‑describedby and does not set aria‑invalid="true". Without aria‑invalid, screen readers cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult() when result.valid is false, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); (and remove it when valid). This aligns with WCAG 2.1 A‑level requirement.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑hidden on the drawer when it is hidden

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu openInstance does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function _openInstance(inst) opens a dropdown panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate calls srUtils.markInvalid which only adds aria‑describedby but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies therefore do not receive the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate replace srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); with srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and ensure setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false') when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: _siteSearch.init selects the search <input> but never gives it a label, aria‑label, or placeholder that conveys purpose. Users of assistive tech cannot identify the field.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining _inputEl, add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.search || 'Search'); or associate a <label for> element in the markup.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: _scrollToTop.init selects the scroll‑to‑top button but does not give it an aria‑label or visible text. The button is therefore invisible to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After _btn is obtained, add setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top'); or include visible text inside the button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: _pricing._updateSavingsMessage updates the text of _savingsMsg but the element lacks role="status" or aria‑live, so changes are not announced to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _pricing.init or _updateSavingsMessage, add setAttr(_savingsMsg, 'role', 'status'); and setAttr(_savingsMsg, 'aria-live', 'polite'); so the message is spoken when it appears.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: _pricing._toggleEl (the element with data‑billing-toggle) is made focusable but never receives an aria‑label or visible text, leaving the control unnamed for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: After _toggleEl is obtained, add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.toggleBilling || 'Toggle billing period'); or provide visible text inside the toggle.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation button missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button is obtained but never given an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` value. Screen readers therefore have no reliable state before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating `_trigger`, set the attribute:  

```js
if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` add/remove the `hidden` attribute on the dropdown panel but never modify the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the associated trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not update aria‑expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` functions expand/collapse FAQ panels but never set `aria‑expanded` on the question button (`item.trigger`). This prevents AT users from knowing the panel state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` which only adds `aria‑describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the field. The HTML `required` attribute is not present on many custom‑validated fields, so AT cannot detect errors.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge()` changes the numeric text of the product count badge but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the new result count after filtering.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new period to AT
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates the UI (price numbers, badges) but does not announce the selected billing period, leaving AT users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After applying the new period, call `liveRegion.announce` with a concise message.  

```js
function setPeriod(period, animate) {
  …
  liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`); // ← added
}
```

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu initial aria‑expanded state not set on page load for some triggers
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()` the code only sets `aria‑expanded="false"` if the attribute is missing **and** the trigger is a `<button>`. However, some triggers are `<a>` elements that act as dropdown toggles; they receive no initial `aria‑expanded` value, violating the requirement that hidden widgets expose their state.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu initial aria‑expanded state not set on page load for some triggers

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visual state of the navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the nav‑toggle button, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') in open() and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') in close().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks aria‑controls linking to the drawer panel
issuesFound.explanation: The nav‑toggle element is used to open/close the navigation drawer but never receives an aria‑controls attribute that references the drawer’s id, leaving the relationship undisclosed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining the drawer element, assign setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id) (or generate an id for the drawer if missing).

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load for the mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the navigation drawer is hidden, yet the nav‑toggle button does not have aria‑expanded="false", violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their collapsed state on load.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() or after selecting _trigger, call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded when toggled via keyboard or click after initial load
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions change the panel visibility but never modify the aria‑expanded state of the question <button>, so assistive technology cannot track which FAQ is open.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close(item) add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: _productFilter._updateCountBadge() updates the numeric text of the count badge but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, leaving users unaware of the number of matching products after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, also update an aria‑live region (e.g., a hidden <div role="status">) with a message like “X products found”.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge lacks aria‑live attribute for dynamic updates
issuesFound.explanation: The element identified by [data-product-count] does not have an aria‑live property, so changes to its content are not automatically announced.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter.init() or when the badge element is created, add setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite'); (and optionally 'aria-atomic', 'true').

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not have role="button" when it is not a <button> element
issuesFound.explanation: If the nav‑toggle selector resolves to an element that is not a native <button> (e.g., a <div> or <span>), it lacks an explicit role="button" and appropriate keyboard handling, making it inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: After selecting _trigger, if !_trigger.matches('button'), call setAttr(_trigger, 'role', 'button'); and ensure it is focusable (tabindex="0").

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not have role="button" when it is not a native <button> element
issuesFound.explanation: The elements used as dropdown triggers may be generic elements without an explicit role="button", which fails to convey their interactive nature to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register() after locating trigger, if !trigger.matches('button'), add setAttr(trigger, 'role', 'button'); and setAttr(trigger, 'tabindex', '0').

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function in the dropdownMenu module adds aria-controls to the trigger button but never sets aria‑expanded when the menu is opened or closed. Screen‑reader users cannot know the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2158: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when the trigger is registered and update it in _openInstance() and _closeInstance() (e.g., setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(inst.open))).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu.register() the trigger button is given aria-controls but never receives an initial aria‑expanded value (defaults to undefined). This violates the requirement that collapsible controls expose their state on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') immediately after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected via aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields as invalid by adding an error message element, but it never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the input, nor clears it on valid input. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3024: After if (!result.valid) add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and in the else branch removeAttribute('aria-invalid') or set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product‑count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge() updates the numeric badge but does not announce the new count via a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2678: Create a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> and set its textContent to `${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} found` each time the count changes.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates plan prices but does not provide an audible cue that the billing period has changed, which is important for users relying on screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: After updating the UI, call liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`).

issuesFound.title: FAQ search results count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion._filterByQuery() updates the visible FAQ items and the count of matches, but it only announces a generic message via liveRegion.announce without providing the exact number of results, which can be ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4172: Replace the generic message with liveRegion.announce(`${count} question${count!==1?'s':''} found for "${query}"`).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in multi‑step wizard not announced
issuesFound.explanation: multiStepWizard._onNext() advances to the next step and updates the progress bar, but does not announce the step change to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4539: After _goToStep() or _updateProgress(), add liveRegion.announce(`Step ${_current+1} of ${_steps.length}: ${_steps[_current].title}`).

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone missing aria‑describedby for status messages
issuesFound.explanation: fileUploadField.bind() creates a status element _statusEl but does not associate it with the upload zone via aria‑describedby, so screen‑reader users may miss upload status.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3321: After creating _statusEl, call setAttr(_zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', _statusEl.id).

issuesFound.title: Missing role="status" on toast notifications
issuesFound.explanation: toastManager.show() creates a toast element with role="alert" but does not set aria‑live or role="status" to ensure the message is announced in all browsers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: Add setAttr(toast, 'aria-live', 'assertive') or change role="alert" to role="status" with aria‑live="assertive".

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing aria‑expanded (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` is given `role="combobox"` and `aria‑autocomplete`, but its expanded/collapsed state is never reflected via `aria‑expanded`. Users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the suggestion list is visible.

issuesFound.title: Suggestion‑list renderer does not set aria‑expanded on the input (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.mount()` creates the `<ul role="listbox">` and wires ARIA attributes, but the associated input never receives `aria‑expanded` to indicate the listbox visibility.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger button missing aria‑expanded (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are parsed with `aria-controls` but the trigger never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute, nor is it updated when the answer panel opens or closes. This breaks the accordion pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑pressed (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`_periodBtns`) are toggles but never receive `aria‑pressed` to convey their selected state. Screen‑reader users cannot know which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch missing aria‑pressed (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives `aria‑pressed` to indicate its on/off state, leaving the switch’s state ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `pricing.init()` after creating `_toggleEl`, set the initial attribute:

```javascript
setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));
```

and in `_onToggleClick()` (or wherever the period changes) update it:

```javascript
setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));
```

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on error (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect validation errors.

fpTitles: FAQ trigger button missing aria‑expanded (JS‑E)

issuesFound.title: Loading button in NewsletterForm missing aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used in NewsletterForm receives a loading prop (which the component does not recognise) instead of isLoading. Consequently aria‑busy is never set and screen‑reader users are not informed that the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 761: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>

issuesFound.title: Loading button in ContactForm missing aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> in ContactForm uses the non‑existent loading prop, so isLoading remains false and the component does not add aria‑busy. Assistive technology therefore does not know the button is in a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1062: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>

issuesFound.title: contact‑name input aria‑describedby points to missing element

issuesFound.fix: Line 904: aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? undefined : undefined}

issuesFound.title: contact‑subject input aria‑describedby points to missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Subject” includes aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" without a corresponding element, preventing screen readers from reading the error.

issuesFound.title: contact‑message textarea aria‑describedby points to missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for “Message” builds aria-describedby from counterId and contact-message-error, but the error id does not exist, breaking the reference for error announcements.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop <div> has onClick without role/button and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div> closes the mobile navigation on click but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and an onKeyDown handler, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 511: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose}> → <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> has onClick without role/button and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay <div> closes the dialog on click but is not focusable nor announced as a button, lacking role="button", tabIndex, and an onKeyDown handler, which prevents keyboard users from dismissing the modal via the overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}> → <div className="modal-overlay" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup missing error props
issuesFound.explanation: The <FormGroup> in NewsletterForm receives an error prop that <FormGroup> does not recognise, so no <ErrorMessage> is rendered. The <TextInput> still references aria‑describedby="newsletter-email-error", which therefore points to a non‑existent element, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
```tsx
{/* Original line 1128 */}
<FormGroup
  label="Email address"
  htmlFor="newsletter-email"
  error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
>
{/* Replace with */}
<FormGroup
  label="Email address"
  htmlFor="newsletter-email"
  errorId="newsletter-email-error"
  errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
>
```

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm Submit button loading state not communicated
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used for the newsletter submit passes loading={isSubmitting}, but <Button> expects the prop isLoading. Consequently aria‑busy is never set, so assistive technology cannot perceive the loading state.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup missing error props for Name field
issuesFound.explanation: The <FormGroup> for Full name passes error={…} instead of errorId and errorMessage, so no <ErrorMessage> is rendered and the <TextInput> references a non‑existent contact-name-error ID.
issuesFound.fix:
```tsx
{/* Original line 1245 */}

  label="Full name"
  htmlFor="contact-name"
  required
  error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
>
{/* Replace with */}
<FormGroup
  label="Full name"
  htmlFor="contact-name"
  required
  errorId="contact-name-error"
  errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
>
```

>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup missing error props for Subject field

issuesFound.title: ContactForm FormGroup missing error props for Message field

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Name input missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor
issuesFound.explanation: The <label> inside the <FormGroup> component does not have an htmlFor attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot associate the label with its input. This affects every form that uses <FormGroup>.
issuesFound.fix:
```tsx
// Line 311
<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">
```

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls the visibility of the submenu but does not expose its expanded state via aria‑expanded, leaving AT users unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Each mobile navigation section button toggles a submenu but lacks aria‑expanded, so assistive technologies cannot convey the open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual toggle buttons indicate selection visually but do not expose the state via aria‑pressed, preventing AT users from knowing which period is active.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as a toggle group but lack aria‑pressed, so the selected filter is not announced to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens/closes the off‑canvas mobile navigation panel does not indicate its expanded state via aria‑expanded, so AT users cannot tell whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme‑toggle button renders only an icon (☀️/🌙/💻) without an aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, violating the rule for icon‑only interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button inside the mobile navigation panel contains only an “✕” character and lacks an aria‑label, making it invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
```tsx
// Line 1249
<IconButton
  aria-label="Close navigation menu"
  onClick={onClose}
  className="mobile-nav__close"
>
  ✕
</IconButton>
```

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev/next IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation:
Both carousel navigation buttons contain only arrow characters and have no aria‑label, so AT users cannot discern their purpose.  
Fix (prev button): ```tsx
// Line 1509
<IconButton
  aria-label="Previous testimonial"
  aria-controls={trackId}
  onClick={handlePrev}
  className="testimonials-track__prev"
>
  ‹
</IconButton>
```  
Fix (next button): ```tsx
// Line 1520
<IconButton
  aria-label="Next testimonial"
  aria-controls={trackId}
  onClick={handleNext}
  className="testimonials-track__next"
>
  ›
</IconButton>
```

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline) in the same rule block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements in standard viewing modes, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline) in the same rule block. Combined with the global focus reset, buttons have no visible focus state for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. The pseudo-element click area does not provide a focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper sets outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when the table region is focused for scrolling.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify the active toggle.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which question is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. The accent-color does not provide a focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus state in the footer.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot navigate the footer links visibly.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which social icon is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule inside @media (forced-colors: active) sets outline: none without a visible alternative. This removes focus indicators for users in high-contrast mode.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons require larger touch targets for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size (and 24px minimum). It lacks sufficient padding to compensate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. While the label is larger, the input itself should meet size requirements for direct interaction.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform which causes motion. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 413 is empty and does not disable this transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element uses transition: transform for a 3D effect. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 833 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative (such as box-shadow or border) in the same rule block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements unless specifically overridden, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on button components
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple button and interactive selectors (e.g., .btn, .nav-toggle, .filter-tabs button) set outline: none on :focus-visible without defining a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same block. Keyboard users cannot see focus on these controls.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Form input selectors (#search-input, .form-group input, etc.) set outline: none on :focus-visible. While :focus has a box-shadow, :focus-visible overrides it on keyboard focus without redefining the shadow, hiding the focus state for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on link components
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple link selectors (e.g., .feature-card a, footer nav a, .social-links a) set outline: none on :focus-visible without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify which link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which renders content visibly on the page instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-primary uses #5588bb text on a #ffffff background (inherited). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.8:1, which fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text, making the button label hard to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on outline and ghost buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-outline and .btn-ghost use #5588bb text on a transparent/white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.8:1, failing WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 513: .btn-outline { background-color: transparent; border-color: #003888; color: #003888; }
Line 523: .btn-ghost { background-color: transparent; border-color: transparent; color: #003888; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-danger uses #ee9999 background with #ffffff text. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.75:1, which critically fails WCAG AA (4.5:1), making the text illegible for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: .btn-danger { background-color: #991b1b; border-color: #991b1b; color: #ffffff; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: .feature-card a uses #5588bb text on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.8:1, failing WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: .feature-card a { color: #003888; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon sets min-width and min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (Target Size Minimum AA). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 508: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm sets min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px. Small touch targets are difficult to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width and height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Custom-styled form controls must meet target size requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1148: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .skip-link and .btn use transition: transform ... which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disabling these specific transitions, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: (Add to existing media query) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, which can significantly degrade readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 213: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative indicator
issuesFound.explanation: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a and .social-links a remove text-decoration (underline) and rely solely on colour change on hover to indicate interactivity. Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 658: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }
Line 1206: .social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

fpTitles: Insufficient colour contrast on outline and ghost buttons

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing keyboard support and role
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (_btn) is bound only to a click event in scrollToTop.init. If the HTML element is not a native <button>, keyboard users cannot activate it with Enter or Space. The code also does not enforce role="button" or tabindex="0" if the element is a div or span.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2395: if (!_btn.hasAttribute('tabindex')) _btn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if (!_btn.getAttribute('role')) _btn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); _btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onClick(); } });

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle missing keyboard support and role
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher delegates click events to [data-theme-toggle] elements in themeSwitcher.init. It does not bind a keydown handler for Enter/Space, nor does it ensure the elements have role="button" or tabindex="0" if they are not native buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5695: document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { const btn = e.target.closest('[data-theme-toggle]'); if (btn && (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ')) { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { if (!btn.hasAttribute('tabindex')) btn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if (!btn.getAttribute('role')) btn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); });

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing keyboard support and role
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (_clearBtn) is bound only to a click event in siteSearch.init. If the HTML element is not a native <button>, keyboard users cannot activate it. The code does not enforce role="button" or tabindex="0".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1725: if (_clearBtn) { if (!_clearBtn.hasAttribute('tabindex')) _clearBtn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if (!_clearBtn.getAttribute('role')) _clearBtn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); _clearBtn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onClearClick(e); } }); _clearBtn.addEventListener('click', _onClearClick); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing Enter/Space support and role enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav trigger (_trigger) has a keydown listener in mobileNav.init, but it only handles Escape. It does not handle Enter or Space to toggle the menu. It also does not enforce role="button" or tabindex="0" if the trigger is not a native button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1960: if (!_trigger.hasAttribute('tabindex')) _trigger.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if (!_trigger.getAttribute('role')) _trigger.setAttribute('role', 'button'); _trigger.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onTriggerClick(); } else if (e.key === KEYS.ESCAPE) { if (_open) { e.preventDefault(); close(); } } });

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing Enter/Space activation via Roving Tabindex
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period buttons use createRovingTabindex with activateOnFocus: false in pricing.init. This relies on click events. If the elements are not native buttons, Enter/Space will not activate them. The roving tabindex controller does not add this handler automatically when activateOnFocus is false.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2735: _rovingPeriodBtns = createRovingTabindex(periodContainer, { itemSelector : '[data-period]', orientation  : 'horizontal', activateOnFocus : true, });

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard navigation buttons missing keyboard support and role
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard Prev/Next buttons (_prevBtn, _nextBtn) are bound only to click events in multiStepWizard.init. They lack keydown handlers for Enter/Space and do not enforce role="button" or tabindex="0" if they are not native buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3435: [_prevBtn, _nextBtn].forEach(btn => { if (btn) { if (!btn.hasAttribute('tabindex')) btn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if (!btn.getAttribute('role')) btn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); } });

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel controls missing keyboard support and role
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel Play, Prev, and Next buttons (_playBtn, _prevBtn, _nextBtn) are bound only to click events in testimonialsCarousel.init. They lack keydown handlers for Enter/Space and do not enforce role="button" or tabindex="0" if they are not native buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3715: [_playBtn, _prevBtn, _nextBtn].forEach(btn => { if (btn) { if (!btn.hasAttribute('tabindex')) btn.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if (!btn.getAttribute('role')) btn.setAttribute('role', 'button'); btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } }); } });

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing role and tabindex enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets ARIA attributes on the trigger but does not enforce role="button" or tabindex="0" if the trigger element is not a native <button>. This makes it inaccessible to keyboard users if implemented as a div or span.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2135: if (trigger.tagName !== 'BUTTON' && trigger.tagName !== 'A') { if (!trigger.hasAttribute('tabindex')) trigger.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); if (!trigger.getAttribute('role')) trigger.setAttribute('role', 'button'); }
