issuesFound.title: File upload validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_addFile` function in `fileUploadField` validates files via `_validateFile`. If validation fails, it announces the error via `liveRegion` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element. Screen readers may announce the message but the input's programmatic state remains valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: `binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in `statCounterAnimator` updates the text content of stat elements when they scroll into view. While it updates `aria-label` on completion, it does not announce the new value via a live region. Users may miss the updated statistic entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3192: `liveRegion.announce(\`Stat updated to $\{cfg.prefix\}$\{formatted\}$\{cfg.suffix\}\`);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter may cause focus loss when hiding cards
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function in `productFilter` hides cards that no longer match the filter (`toHideEls`). If the currently focused element is one of these cards, focus is lost when `hidden` is applied. There is no check to move focus to a logical alternative (e.g., filter tabs or count badge).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: `if (toHideEls.includes(document.activeElement)) { qs('[data-filter-tabs]')?.focus(); }`

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher lacks keyboard support for non-button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `themeSwitcher` attaches a `click` listener to `[data-theme-toggle]`. If these elements are not native `<button>`s (e.g., `<div>`), keyboard users cannot activate them. There is no delegated `keydown` handler for Enter/Space.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3490: `document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.target.matches('[data-theme-toggle]') && (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ')) { e.preventDefault(); e.target.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel causes focus loss on slide change
issuesFound.explanation: The `goTo` function in `testimonialsCarousel` sets `tabindex="-1"` on inactive slides. If a user has focused a slide (via tab), and the slide changes (auto-play or control), that element becomes unfocusable and focus is lost. Focus should be managed to stay on controls or move to the new slide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2642: `if (document.activeElement === prevSlide.el) { _nextBtn?.focus() || _prevBtn?.focus(); }`

issuesFound.title: Inline validator error element has redundant ARIA live attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The `_getOrCreateErrorEl` function in `inlineValidator` creates an error element with `role="alert"` (which implies `aria-live="assertive"`) but also explicitly sets `aria-live="polite"`. This redundancy can confuse assistive technologies about the politeness level of the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2434: `removeAttribute('aria-live')` (rely on role="alert" for assertiveness)

issuesFound.title: Toggle switch uses invalid aria-checked on button element
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle element (_toggleEl) is assigned aria-checked="true/false" regardless of its tag name. If the HTML provides a <button> element for the toggle, aria-checked is invalid (buttons should use aria-pressed). The code only assigns role="switch" if the tag is NOT a button, but still applies aria-checked to buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: if (_toggleEl.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)); } else { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); }

issuesFound.title: Period buttons have conflicting ARIA states (aria-pressed and aria-selected)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the _periodBtns loop, the code sets both aria-pressed and aria-selected on the same button elements. These attributes represent different widgets (toggle button vs tab/listbox item) and should not be used together on the same element as it confuses assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: Remove setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); and rely solely on aria-pressed for toggle buttons, OR ensure the container has role="tablist" and buttons have role="tab" and use only aria-selected.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent trigger does not indicate dialog state
issuesFound.explanation: When the [data-cookie-prefs] button is clicked to open the consent banner (a dialog), the button's state is not updated. It lacks aria-haspopup="dialog" and does not toggle aria-expanded="true", so screen reader users are not informed that a dialog has opened from this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In _onPrefsClick, add: btn.setAttribute('aria-haspopup', 'dialog'); btn.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); (and reverse on close).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module emits custom events but does not write to the liveRegion when a menu opens or closes. Unlike mobileNav or faqAccordion, users are not audibly informed of the state change, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In _openInstance, add: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); In _closeInstance, add: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animations update content without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator updates el.textContent dynamically to count up numbers. This change is not contained within an aria-live region nor announced via liveRegion.announce, so screen reader users miss the updated values.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: Wrap the number element in a container with aria-live="polite" OR add liveRegion.announce(_format(cfg.target, cfg)) in the onComplete callback.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone creates double tab stop with native input
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets tabindex="0" on the custom drop zone (zoneEl) to make it focusable, but does not hide the native <input type="file"> (inputEl) from the tab order. This results in two focusable elements triggering the same file picker action.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: After const inputEl = qs(...), add: inputEl.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'); inputEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button aria-pressed not initialized if autoPlay is false
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button's aria-pressed state is only set inside the play() and pause() functions. If CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay is false, neither is called during init, leaving the button without an initial aria-pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: After init logic, add: if (_playBtn) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', _playing ? 'true' : 'false'); }

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides use aria-roledescription without base role
issuesFound.explanation: The code sets aria-roledescription="slide" on slide elements. This attribute is ignored by assistive technologies unless the element also has a valid implicit or explicit base role (e.g., role="group" or role="listitem"), which is not set by the JS.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: When setting aria-roledescription, also ensure a base role exists: slide.el.setAttribute('role', 'group'); slide.el.setAttribute('aria-roledescription', 'slide');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded, while the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false".

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The paired _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. The markValid function correctly removes the attribute, but the invalid state is never programmatically asserted, preventing screen readers from announcing the error status.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2672: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.fix:
Line 1638: liveRegion.announce('Submenu opened');
  Line 1655: liveRegion.announce('Submenu closed');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent decision not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel init does not initialize play button state when autoplay disabled

issuesFound.title: srUtils auditImages sets empty alt text on potentially informative images
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.auditImages function automatically sets alt="" on any image missing an alt attribute. This treats potentially informative images as decorative, hiding their content from screen reader users instead of flagging the error for repair.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2594: // img.setAttribute('alt', '');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function sets the drawer to visible but fails to update the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to "true". The corresponding `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers announce the menu as closed even when it is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set the trigger's `aria-expanded` attribute to "true". The `_closeInstance` function sets it to "false", causing assistive technology to misreport the dropdown state as collapsed when expanded.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but omits setting `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger. The `close` function sets it to "false", resulting in incorrect state announcement for screen reader users when an item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates fails to update aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function updates the visual active class on filter buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` (or `aria-checked`) attribute. Keyboard users relying on state attributes will not know which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true" on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. This prevents assistive technology from identifying the field as invalid during form validation.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner does not announce dialog opening via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function displays the cookie consent dialog and traps focus, but does not announce the dialog's appearance to screen readers via a live region. Users may be confused by the sudden focus change without context.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._addFile does not set aria-invalid on input when validation fails
issuesFound.explanation: When a selected file fails validation (size/type), the function announces the error but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the file input element. The input state remains valid programmatically despite the error.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply does not set aria-invalid on input when code is invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a discount code validation fails, the function announces the error message but does not update the input's `aria-invalid` attribute to "true". Screen readers will not identify the input field itself as containing an error.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds classes and announces state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function animates the panel and updates classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will not announce the answer as expanded.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._onTabClick function does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _onTabClick handler updates the filter state and CSS class but does not set aria-pressed="true" on the selected button or "false" on others. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 for name, role, value.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, only that it has a description.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded state

issuesFound.title: Skip link target #main-nav missing on mobile
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link references #main-nav, which is the ID of the DesktopNav component. On mobile viewports, DesktopNav is not rendered (conditional rendering at Line 615), so this ID does not exist in the DOM. Keyboard users on mobile who activate this skip link will lose focus or be directed to a non-existent target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 395: {SITE.skipToNav}

issuesFound.title: Skip link target #site-search missing on mobile

On mobile viewports, SearchInput is not rendered (conditional rendering at Line 780), so this ID does not exist in the DOM. Keyboard users on mobile who activate this skip link will lose focus or be directed to a non-existent target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 396: {SITE.skipToSearch}

issuesFound.title: NotificationContext function name mismatch
issuesFound.explanation: The NotificationContainer component destructures dismiss from the context, but the NotificationProvider exposes removeNotification. This causes the dismiss button in toast notifications to fail (TypeError or no-op), preventing users from dismissing alerts, which is critical for role="alert" regions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1517: const { notifications, removeNotification } = useContext(NotificationContext);
  Line 1522: <NotificationToast key={n.id} notification={n} onDismiss={removeNotification} />

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". Assistive technologies rely on aria-modal being on the dialog element to correctly suppress background content. Placing it on the overlay may result in inconsistent behavior across screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1453: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 1455: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()} >

issuesFound.title: Focus restoration relies on unattached ref
issuesFound.explanation: The closeDemoModal function attempts to restore focus to demoTriggerRef.current, but this ref is never attached to any DOM element because SiteHeader does not accept or use the demoTriggerRef prop. This results in focus loss when the modal closes, as the ref is null.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1644: // Remove manual focus restoration; useFocusTrap handles this automatically
  Line 1644: requestAnimationFrame(() => demoTriggerRef.current?.focus());

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader receives undefined props blocking modal trigger
issuesFound.explanation: LuminaryPage passes onRequestDemo and demoTriggerRef to SiteHeader, but SiteHeader's interface does not define these props and they are ignored. Consequently, there is no trigger in the UI to open the demo modal, making the modal functionality completely inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1633: <SiteHeader />

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border in the same rule block. This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users on all interactive elements that do not have specific focus styles overridden elsewhere, potentially making it impossible to see where focus is located.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on button focus
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border in the same rule block. This removes the focus indicator for buttons when reached via keyboard navigation, making it difficult for keyboard-only users to identify which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on primary button class
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum for touch targets. This may make the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum for touch targets. This may make the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets explicit width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the button extremely difficult or impossible to activate for many users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the recommended 44px for comfortable touch activation. This may make the links difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide the content. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, breaking the visual layout and potentially confusing sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 395) and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active (line 413), causing spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who have requested reduced motion, which may cause vestibular disorders or discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition (line 778) and applies transform: translateY(-1px) on hover (line 784), causing spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation menu hover state
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state for navigation links sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#005fcc) on background: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.14:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text, making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation menu current page indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The current page state for navigation links sets color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#005fcc) on background: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.14:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to identify the current page.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with only colour change on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a sets text-decoration: none and provides only a colour change (to var(--colour-brand-primary)) on hover without additional non-colour visual indicators such as underline, border-bottom, or background change. issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Since the base .btn class does not define an alternative focus indicator, keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px minimum recommended by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility removes content from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, clip: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. A correct implementation requires position: absolute, small dimensions (1px × 1px), and clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class defines transition: transform var(--transition-base), which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion, potentially causing vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 424-429) and defines transform: translateY(1px) on :active (line 443). issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button hover

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target potentially too small
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size and has no surrounding padding to compensate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 449: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text will be visible to all users, breaking the layout and potentially exposing text meant only for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383-393: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (for the :active state), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 534: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 370) .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transition on transform (perspective rotation), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 595 is empty and does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595-598: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Product card transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card selector includes transform in its transition (translateY on hover), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 763: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 854 or create new) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Skip link transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes a transition on transform (translateY), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 370) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at certain zoom levels.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1783: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons define transition properties including transform transitions but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable or reduce these animations. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from these animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 526: (add after existing .btn rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images define transform and box-shadow transitions but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 975 is empty and does not disable these transitions. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the 3D transform animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 975: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses background-color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999) with color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails WCAG 2.1 AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision may not be able to read the button text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 587: .btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .newsletter-section
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section uses white text (var(--colour-text-on-dark) #ffffff) on a gradient background ending at var(--colour-brand-secondary) (#bb88ee). The contrast ratio between white and #bb88ee is approximately 2.1:1, which fails WCAG 2.1 AA requirements of 4.5:1. Users with low vision may not be able to read the text over the lighter portions of the gradient.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1700: .newsletter-section { padding-block: var(--section-gap); background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--colour-brand-primary) 0%, var(--colour-brand-secondary) 100%); color: var(--colour-text-on-dark); position: relative; } (add overlay) .newsletter-section::before { content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.4); z-index: 0; } .newsletter-section > * { position: relative; z-index: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover or border-bottom) for many link instances. Users with color vision deficiencies may not be able to distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add specific hover underlines to link selectors that lack them.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers on .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and other properties that make the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This prevents screen reader users from accessing content intended for them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 444: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Form label hidden from screen readers on #site-search label.sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The search form label uses the .sr-only class but is then overridden with display: none, which removes the element from the accessibility tree entirely. Screen reader users will not hear the label for the search input, making it difficult to understand the field's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 772: #site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .step-number

issuesFound.fix: Line ~906: `.step-number { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 56px; height: 56px; flex-shrink: 0; background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--colour-brand-primary), var(--colour-brand-secondary)); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-extrabold); border-radius: var(--radius-full); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); line-height: 1; user-select: none; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-labelledby reference on [role="group"][aria-labelledby="filter-label"]
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [aria-labelledby="filter-label"] references an ID "filter-label" that does not exist in the document (only the class .filter-label is defined), causing assistive technologies to fail to calculate the accessible name for the filter group.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~772: Change `[role="group"][aria-labelledby="filter-label"]` to `[role="group"][aria-labelledby="filter-label-id"]` and ensure the corresponding HTML element has `id="filter-label-id"`, or remove the aria-labelledby if the label is visually present and associated via other means.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed on global a selector
issuesFound.explanation: Global removal of text-decoration from all anchor elements without providing an alternative non-color visual differentiator in the default state violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as links may be indistinguishable from surrounding text for colorblind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~312: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative visual indicator such as border-bottom or font-weight differentiation.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed on #nav-menu > li > a

issuesFound.fix: Line ~604: `#nav-menu > li > a { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 28px; }` and add hover underline: `#nav-menu > li > a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-info-light); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.fix: Line ~681: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { display: block; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 28px; display: flex; align-items: center; }` and add: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:hover { background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100); color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed on .social-links a

issuesFound.title: scroll-to-top button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is hidden and shown via script, but the code never ensures it has an accessible name (aria-label). Screen-reader users will hear only "button" or silence when focus lands on this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2444: Insert `_btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');` immediately after `_btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobile navigation trigger lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger trigger button for the mobile menu is assigned aria-expanded and aria-controls, but the script never guarantees an accessible name. If the HTML lacks inner text or an aria-label, assistive technologies will announce only "button".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2108: After `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);` add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu');`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle lacks accessible name when not a native button
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing-period toggle is not a `<button>`, the script adds role="switch" and tabindex="0", but fails to provide an aria-label. Users navigating with a screen reader will encounter a control with no name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3406: After `if (!_toggleEl.getAttribute('role')) _toggleEl.setAttribute('role', 'switch');` add `if (!_toggleEl.getAttribute('aria-label')) _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.switchToAnnual || 'Switch billing period');`

issuesFound.title: search clear button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search input’s “clear” button is queried and bound to a click handler, but the script does not ensure it has an accessible name. Icon-only clear buttons without text or aria-label are invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2936: After `_clearBtn = qs(cfg.clearSelector);` add `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) _clearBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: testimonials carousel dots container missing tablist role
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel creates individual dot buttons with role="tab", but the parent container (_dotsEl) is never assigned role="tablist". Assistive technologies cannot correctly interpret the tab structure without the required parent role.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3738: Inside `_buildDots()`, before appending dots, add `if (_dotsEl && !_dotsEl.getAttribute('role')) _dotsEl.setAttribute('role', 'tablist');`

issuesFound.title: multi-step wizard misuses tabpanel role without tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard step panels are given role="tabpanel", yet there are no elements with role="tab" controlling them (only generic “Previous/Next” buttons). This creates an orphaned tabpanel relationship that confuses screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4388: Remove `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` and `step.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-label', step.title);` from the wizard init, or implement a proper tablist with role="tab" buttons for each step.

issuesFound.title: filter tabs use aria-pressed instead of aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The product-filter tab bar is documented as an “ARIA tab pattern” and uses roving tabindex, yet the script sets aria-pressed (meant for toggle buttons) rather than aria-selected (required for tabs). This causes assistive technologies to report the wrong control type and state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3108: In `_syncTabAriaStates()`, replace `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` with `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and ensure each tab has `role="tab"` by adding `if (!btn.getAttribute('role')) btn.setAttribute('role', 'tab');` in the same loop.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard progress bar missing required role
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard component selects an element via `[data-wizard-progress]` and sets `aria-valuenow` and `aria-valuetext` on it to indicate progress. However, the code never ensures the element has `role="progressbar"`. Without this role, assistive technologies will not recognize the element as a progress indicator, causing the ARIA state updates to be ignored.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3947: Add after `_progressBar = qs('[data-wizard-progress]', _form);`
  `if (_progressBar && !_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) _progressBar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar');`

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison highlighter uses aria-selected on invalid elements
issuesFound.explanation: The planComparisonHighlighter.highlightColumn function sets `aria-selected="true"` on elements matching `[data-plan-head]`. If these elements are table header cells (`<th>`) or other elements without an appropriate role (such as `role="tab"` or `role="option"`), `aria-selected` is not a valid attribute for them. This results in invalid ARIA markup that may cause assistive technologies to ignore the selection state or behave unpredictably.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3848: Replace `setAttr(th, 'aria-selected', 'true');` with `setAttr(th, 'aria-current', 'true');`
  Line 3850: Replace `th.removeAttribute('aria-selected');` with `th.removeAttribute('aria-current');`

fpTitles: Multi-step wizard progress bar missing required role
Plan comparison highlighter uses aria-selected on invalid elements

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation does not set aria-invalid on input field
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the error message is displayed in a status element, but the input field itself (data-discount-input) is not marked with aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the field contains an invalid value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3665: Add `srUtils.markInvalid(_inputEl, _statusEl);` after setting the error message. Line 3672: Add `srUtils.markValid(_inputEl);` when validation succeeds.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button lacks guaranteed accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (selected by SELECTORS.navToggle) is ensured to have aria-expanded and aria-controls, but the JavaScript does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label). If the HTML lacks text content or an aria-label attribute, the button will be announced as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2255: Add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open navigation menu'); }`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button lacks guaranteed accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (selected by SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn) is initialized and hidden, but the script does not ensure it has an accessible name. If the HTML element lacks text content or aria-label, screen readers will not announce its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2505: Add `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { setAttr(_btn, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top'); }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons lack guaranteed accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code ensures ARIA expanded, controls, and haspopup attributes, but does not verify or set an accessible name. If the trigger button contains only an icon or lacks text, it will have no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2405: Add `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-label') && !trigger.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(trigger, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.labels.openMenu || 'Open menu'); }`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel root lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel root element is given aria-roledescription="carousel", but the JavaScript does not ensure it has an accessible label (aria-label or aria-labelledby). Screen readers will announce it only as "carousel" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4305: Add `if (!_rootEl.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_rootEl.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) { setAttr(_rootEl, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.testimonialsLabel || 'Customer testimonials'); }`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard form lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard form element is initialized and steps are created, but the script does not ensure the form or wizard container has an accessible name to identify the purpose of the multi-step process.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4555: Add `if (!_form.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_form.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) { setAttr(_form, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.wizardLabel || 'Registration wizard'); }`

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput input lacks guaranteed accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code input field is queried but not guaranteed to have an accessible name. If the HTML lacks a label or aria-label, screen reader users will not know the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3655: Add `if (!_inputEl.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_inputEl.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) { setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.LABEL, STRINGS.discountCodeLabel || 'Discount code'); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer behaves as a modal dialog (traps focus, blocks interaction with the page), but the JavaScript does not set aria-modal="true" on the drawer element. Screen readers may not announce it as a modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: Add `if (_drawer) { setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.MODAL, 'true'); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets the internal _open flag and toggles CSS classes to show the navigation drawer, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1763: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function updates the open state and shows the panel, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. The corresponding _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry that confuses assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1958: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function for FAQ items updates the item state and panel visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", so screen readers will incorrectly report the item as collapsed even when open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2910: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close (via click, keyboard, or hover), no announcement is made via the liveRegion. Users relying on screen readers may not be aware that the dropdown panel has appeared or disappeared, particularly when the interaction is triggered by mouse hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1958 (after panel shown): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` Line 1980 (after panel hidden): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils adds the error message element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine that the field contains an error, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it when the menu opens. Consequently, keyboard focus is not confined to the navigation drawer when open, allowing focus to move to background elements in violation of WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: After `_initialised = true;` add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` Line 1763: After `_open = true;` add `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function deactivates the focus trap (if it exists) but does not explicitly return focus to the trigger button when the menu closes. Combined with Issue 7, this causes focus to be lost to the document body, forcing keyboard users to tab from the beginning of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800 (end of close function, before the emit call): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

fpTitles: Mobile navigation close does not return focus to trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute remains unchanged (or undefined if not initialized), failing to inform assistive technologies that the controlled panel is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1924: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` and before `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion items expand visually via animateHeight, but the trigger button lacks an updated aria-expanded attribute. The close function sets it to "false", but the open function never sets it to "true", leaving screen reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3424: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox opens when the input receives focus or input, but the input element's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true" in the _open function. Although the hide function sets it to "false", the open state is never announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2894: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;` and before the `if (!_inputEl.value)` check.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid utility updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid, and the valid state (which removes the attribute) is not properly contrasted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` line within the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never instantiated or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never assigns createFocusTrap to it. Consequently, when the drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the navigation, allowing keyboard users to tab into the background content behind the drawer.

issuesFound.title: pricing._updatePlanCard does not announce price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period toggles between monthly and annual, the price text content updates visually via textContent assignment, but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users do not receive feedback that the price has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3150: After `plan.priceDisplay.textContent = target;` (and inside the onComplete of animateNumber), add:
```
liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} for ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);
```

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for first name, last name, email, and company are bound for validation but never receive autocomplete attributes (e.g., "given-name", "family-name", "email", "organization"). This prevents browsers from autofill and violates WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.

fpTitles: Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. The `close()` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1528: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function for FAQ items animates the panel open and removes the `hidden` attribute, but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets it to `false`. This leaves screen reader users unaware when an accordion item has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2518: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks focus trap and initial focus
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes or activates a focus trap when opening. Additionally, `open()` does not move focus into the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page while it is open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1532: After `_lockScroll();`, add focus trap initialization and activation:
  Line 1532: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: _drawer, returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line 1533: `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager creates a timer that automatically dismisses toast notifications after a configurable duration (`dur`). There is no mechanism to pause, stop, or hide this auto-updating content, violating WCAG 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide. Users with cognitive disabilities or using screen readers may not have sufficient time to consume the notification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1178: Replace the simple setTimeout with a pausable timer that clears on mouseenter/focusin and restarts on mouseleave/focusout, or provide an explicit pause button. Minimum fix: `let timerId = null; const startTimer = () => { if(dur>0) timerId = setTimeout(()=>dismiss(toast),dur); }; const pauseTimer = () => clearTimeout(timerId); toast.addEventListener('mouseenter',pauseTimer); toast.addEventListener('mouseleave',startTimer); startTimer();`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates()` function updates the visual active state of filter tab buttons but does not set `aria-pressed` to reflect the toggle state. Since these buttons act as toggle buttons (filter on/off), they must communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2325: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions in the dropdownMenu module toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies receive no feedback when a dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1617: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 1641: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function in mobileNav removes the open CSS classes and unlocks scroll, but unlike the `open()` function, it does not announce the state change via the live region. This creates an inconsistent experience where opening is announced but closing is silent.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1555: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`, add:
  Line 1556: `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm status region missing aria-live attribute on creation
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletterForm creates its status element dynamically (`_statusEl`), it sets `role` and `class` but does not ensure `aria-live` is set. Although the code later calls `liveRegion.announce` for some messages, the dedicated status element itself lacks the live region attribute needed for its text updates to be automatically announced by assistive technologies when content changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2913: Change the createElement call to include `aria-live`:
  Line 2913: `_statusEl = createElement('div', { class: 'newsletter-status', 'aria-live': 'polite', role: 'status' });`

fpTitles: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, even though the menu starts in a closed state. This violates the requirement that aria-expanded be set on page load for toggle widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated to reflect the open/closed state. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the change in results count is not announced to screen reader users. Users are not informed that filtering has completed or how many products are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price changes visually but are not announced to screen reader users. Users may not realize the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: After updating plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss lacks pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout without providing a mechanism to pause, stop, or hide them. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 for users who need more time to read notifications.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap activation logic error
issuesFound.explanation: The focus trap is only activated when it is already active (`if (_trap && _trap.isActive())`), which prevents the focus trap from being properly engaged when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not contained within the mobile menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: Change `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.activate();` to `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu focus not returned on outside interaction
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus close due to clicking outside or focus moving outside, the focus is not returned to the trigger button. The returnFocus parameter defaults to undefined (falsy) in these calls, causing focus to be lost.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._onDocumentClick: Change `_closeAll();` to `_closeAll(null, true);`
  Line dropdownMenu._onDocumentFocusin: Change `_closeAll();` to `_closeAll(null, true);`
  Or alternatively, modify _closeInstance to default returnFocus to true: `function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus = true)`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen reader users via the live region. Users may not realize that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion.open: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`
  Line faqAccordion.close: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`

fpTitles: toastManager auto-dismiss lacks pause mechanism
dropdownMenu focus not returned on outside interaction

issuesFound.title: Toast notification auto-dismiss timer lacks pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toast manager sets a setTimeout to auto-dismiss notifications after a duration, but provides no mechanism to pause, stop, or extend the timer when the user hovers over or focuses on the toast. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) as users with reading disabilities may not have sufficient time to read the content before it disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2418: Replace `if (dur > 0) { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); }` with `if (dur > 0) { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); const pause = () => clearTimeout(timerId); const resume = () => timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); toast.addEventListener('mouseenter', pause); toast.addEventListener('mouseleave', resume); toast.addEventListener('focusin', pause); toast.addEventListener('focusout', resume); }`

issuesFound.title: Pricing updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the _updatePlanCard function updates the price textContent but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the price has changed dynamically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4870: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target}`);` immediately after `plan.priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-label', target);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter count updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the textContent of the count badge when filters are applied, but does not use a live region to announce the result count to screen reader users. Users are not informed how many products match their selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4470: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` immediately after `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`);`

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing required ARIA role
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateProgress function sets aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext on the progress bar element, but does not ensure the element has role="progressbar". Without the role, assistive technologies may not recognize it as a progress indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5650: Add `if (!_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) { _progressBar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100'); }` at the beginning of the _updateProgress function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions omit aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions show or hide the dropdown panel by adding/removing CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute, but they never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Screen readers will not announce the expanded/collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in `_openInstance`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line (in `_closeInstance`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions omit aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled open and closed, but the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger trigger button (`_trigger`) is never updated to reflect the state. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing whether the menu is open or closed.

Line (in `open`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_open = true;`
  Line (in `close`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable intended for focus management but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap`, nor calls `_trap.activate()` when the menu opens. Consequently, keyboard focus is not confined to the mobile menu when it is open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

Line (in `init`): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons omit aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons indicating the active category, but the function only toggles a CSS class. It does not update `aria-pressed` (or `aria-selected`) to communicate the active state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in `_syncTabAriaStates`): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the `forEach` loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox omits initial aria-expanded

This violates the ARIA combobox pattern which requires the attribute to be present and reflect the state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in `mount`): Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting `aria-haspopup`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not be aware that the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in `setPeriod`, after updating plan cards): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or similar appropriate message based on the period).

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields

issuesFound.fix: Line 1383: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markValid does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the markValid function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not remove or set to false the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1401: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close do not announce state changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions accept an announceToSR parameter that is never used. When the navigation opens or closes, no announcement is made via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1627: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` inside open(). Line 1652: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` inside close().

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually and the toggle state changes, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible card count changes and cards are shown or hidden, but no announcement is made via the live region. Screen reader users are not informed how many products match the selected filter or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3140: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${s.activeCategory} products`);` after the filter animation completes.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close do not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions accept an announceToSR parameter that is never utilized. When FAQ items expand or collapse, screen reader users do not receive confirmation of the action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3552: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title));` inside open(). Line 3572: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title));` inside close().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created or queried during initialization, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially. Since the menu is closed by default, the trigger must explicitly declare its collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1685: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after verifying _trigger exists.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialize aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but it does not initialize aria-expanded="false" even though the panel is hidden by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1795: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting the panel hidden attribute.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialize aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the suggestion panel is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2475: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element is updated with aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext, but the required aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax attributes are never set in the JavaScript, preventing screen readers from calculating the completion percentage.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4222: Add `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');` inside the function (or in the init function).

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes for personal data fields
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form binds validation to fields with IDs #contact-first-name, #contact-last-name, and #contact-email (clear personal data signals), but the JavaScript does not set the autocomplete attribute on these inputs. This prevents browsers from assisting users with autofill and violates WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4410: After selecting each field, add `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');` for first name, `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');` for last name, and `field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');` for email.

fpTitles: multiStepWizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
Contact form inputs missing autocomplete attributes for personal data fields

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button opens and closes the drawer via the open() and close() functions, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute to reflect the current state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 970: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;` in the open function. Line 990: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies may assume the menu is expanded by default or fail to recognize it as a disclosure control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input acts as a combobox that shows and hides a suggestion listbox, but the _open() and _close() functions do not toggle aria-expanded on the input element. Screen readers cannot inform users when the suggestion dropdown is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;` in the _open function. Line 1390: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;` in the _close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is given role="combobox" during initialization, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially. This violates the ARIA combobox pattern which requires the expanded state to be declared on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1500: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown menu, the trigger button receives aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" even though the panel is hidden by default. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` in the register function.

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Conversely, markValid() removes the describedby association but does not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` at the end of the markInvalid function. Line 820: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`) at the end of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count and results change dynamically, but no aria-live announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed how many products match the selected filters or that the grid has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` and before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement for state changes
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the visual state changes and aria-expanded is updated, but screen readers receive no announcement of the state change because there is no live region update. Users may not realize the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} expanded`);` after `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the open function. Line 2600: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} collapsed`);` after `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), the price values update visually with animation, but the new prices are not announced to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology may not perceive that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` after `_syncToggleState(period);` and before the emit call in the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute misplaced on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the outer overlay div instead of the inner div that has role="dialog". This causes assistive technologies to incorrectly identify the modal boundary, potentially allowing users to navigate outside the dialog or failing to announce it as modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2280: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm error message not connected to input
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed an "error" prop, but it expects "errorId" and "errorMessage". This prevents the error message from rendering and leaves the input's aria-describedby attribute referencing a non-existent ID, so screen reader users are not informed of validation errors.

issuesFound.explanation: LuminaryPage passes demoTriggerRef to SiteHeader to enable focus return when the demo modal closes, but SiteHeader does not accept or use this ref. When the modal closes, focus cannot return to the trigger element, violating WCAG focus management requirements.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div has onClick handler without keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The modal backdrop element is a div with an onClick handler to close the modal when clicked. It lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler, making the close-on-click functionality inaccessible to keyboard-only users who cannot use the mouse to click the backdrop.

This incorrectly marks the backdrop as modal instead of the dialog itself, potentially causing assistive technologies to misidentify which element is the modal container.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1852: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the overlay div. Line ~1853: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the dialog div: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce "star" or "asterisk" redundantly because the required state is already conveyed via the VisuallyHidden text and the required attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 933: *

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button has an isLoading state that disables the control, but it does not include aria-busy to indicate to screen readers that the button is actively processing a request.
issuesFound.fix: Line 823: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Spinner decorative ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner component contains a decorative visual element (the ring span) that is not hidden from assistive technology, causing potential double announcements with the status role and aria-label on the parent.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link that represents the current page has a visual indicator (nav-link--current) but lacks aria-current="page", so screen readers cannot identify it as the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active page link in the mobile navigation is styled with a current-state class but does not expose aria-current, preventing screen readers from announcing it as the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1198: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop per its TypeScript interface, but ThemeToggle renders it without one. The button contains only an emoji character (☀️, 🌙, or 💻) and no other text content, leaving screen reader users without an accessible name to identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1221: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in an error state, but the FormGroup component is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID is rendered in the DOM. This creates a broken reference that causes screen readers to announce nothing or stale content for the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2440: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>
